name: Sylheti
notes: Grapheme inventory NOT YET SPECIFIED (stub). Sylheti is written historically in Sylheti Nagri (ꠍꠤꠟꠐꠤ ꠘꠣꠉꠞꠤ), a Brahmic ABUGIDA whose consonant letters carry an inherent vowel unless a vowel sign or hasanta is applied; in modern practice it is far more often written in the Bengali-Assamese (Eastern Nagari) script, likewise an abugida, and occasionally in Latin transliteration. No Brahmic letter-to-IPA map is claimed here. A Tier-A spec would have to declare WHICH script the input is in and resolve the inherent vowel and its deletion (schwa deletion); the modern Bengali-script route is the more useful one for a G2P consumer. Sylheti has also developed lexical tone from lost aspiration, which is not written.
